data.title: China Launches Action Plan to Bolster Foreign Investment
data.description:
China introduced an action plan to boost the country's foreign investment for this year, Xinhua reported Wednesday, citing an issue from the General Office of the State Council.

Under the plan, new opening-up policies will be implemented in industries such as telecommunication, biotechnology and medical services, the report said.

Restrictions on domestic loans for foreign-invested firms will also be lifted to attract more international investments, the report said.

The Ministry of Commerce and the National Development and Reform Commission composed the plan, which was approved at a State Council executive meeting, the report said.

data.title: Baidu Likely to Benefit From Strong AI Business — Market Talk
data.description: Baidu is likely to benefit from strong AI business, say HSBC analysts in a commentary. The tech company's 4Q cloud revenue came in "meaningfully ahead of consensus" on stronger application programming interface demand, the analysts say. HSBC expects this growth momentum to sustain in 2025, with rising AI adoption from customers and cross-selling opportunities for AI infrastructure in the coming months. HSBC maintains a hold rating and raises target price of its ADRs slightly to US$94.00 from US$93.00. The ADRs last traded at US$88.03, while the Hong Kong-listed shares are down 2.7% at HK$85.95. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Whitecap Resources Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Whitecap Resources Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 40 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 49 cents. The mean expectation of two analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 21 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 14 cents to 28 cents per share.

  • Revenue was C$852.40 million​; analysts expected C$795.00 million.

  • Whitecap Resources Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 40 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of C$233.8 million.

  • Whitecap Resources Inc shares had fallen by 3.2% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no negative revisions of earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 11 "strong buy" or "buy," 3 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the oil & gas exploration and production peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Whitecap Resources Inc is C$14.00

data.title: BNB Price Approaches a Key Level—Can It Clear the Hurdle?
data.description:
BNB price is recovering from the $630 support zone. The price is rising and might aim for a fresh move toward the $680 resistance.

  • BNB price is moving higher above the $650 resistance zone.
  • The price is now trading below $660 and the 100-hourly simple moving average.
  • There is a connecting bearish trend line forming with resistance at $655 on the hourly chart of the BNB/USD pair (data source from Binance).
  • The pair must stay above the $650 level to start another increase in the near term.

BNB Price Eyes Fresh Upside

After forming a base above the $630 level, BNB price started a fresh increase. There was a move above the $635 and $640 resistance levels. It even recovered above the $650 level, beating Ethereum and Bitcoin.

There was a decent move above the 50% Fib retracement level of the downward move from the $682 swing high to the $630 low. However, the bears seem to be active near the $655 and $660 levels. There is also a connecting bearish trend line forming with resistance at $655 on the hourly chart of the BNB/USD pair.

The price is now trading below $660 and the 100-hourly simple moving average. If there is a fresh increase, the price could face resistance near the $655 level. The next resistance sits near the $660 level or the 61.8% Fib retracement level of the downward move from the $682 swing high to the $630 low at $662.

A clear move above the $662 zone could send the price higher. In the stated case, BNB price could test $680. A close above the $680 resistance might set the pace for a larger move toward the $700 resistance. Any more gains might call for a test of the $720 level in the near term.

Another Decline?

If BNB fails to clear the $662 resistance, it could start another decline. Initial support on the downside is near the $650 level. The next major support is near the $642 level.

The main support sits at $630. If there is a downside break below the $630 support, the price could drop toward the $612 support. Any more losses could initiate a larger decline toward the $600 level.

Technical Indicators

Hourly MACD – The MACD for BNB/USD is gaining pace in the bullish zone.

Hourly RSI (Relative Strength Index) – The RSI for BNB/USD is currently above the 50 level.

Major Support Levels – $650 and $642.

Major Resistance Levels – $655 and $662.

data.title: Elon Musk is not India’s ideal foreign investor
data.description:
The country's high import levies have failed to get the Tesla boss to make his vehicles locally. New Delhi may be considering concessions and exemptions to entice him instead. But Donald Trump's trade wars leave India with less leverage to wrangle coveted factory jobs from Musk.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @ShritamaBose on X

CONTEXT NEWS

U.S. President Donald Trump told Fox News in an interview aired on February 19 that if Tesla were to build a factory in India to circumvent that country's tariffs, it would be "unfair" to the United States.

Tesla has selected locations for two showrooms in New Delhi and Mumbai, moving closer to its long-delayed plans to sell its electric cars in India, Reuters reported on February 18, citing unnamed sources familiar with the matter.

The electric vehicle maker has posted job ads on professional networking platform LinkedIn for 15 positions across three locations in India since February 18.

data.title: Shin Shin Names Chairman
data.description:
Shin Shin appointed Lee Wen Ying as the company's chairman following a board resolution on Wednesday, according to a same-day Taiwan Exchange filing.

Lee succeeds You Zhi Huang in the role, the construction machinery company said.

data.title: Turkey - Factors To Watch on February 20
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Thursday.

The lira was quoted at 36.3200-36.3245 against the U.S. dollar at 0450 GMT, after closing at 36.2975 on Wednesday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 1.55% lower at 9,763 points on Wednesday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian shares fell sharply on Thursday, tracking choppy trading on Wall Street overnight and a dip in European stocks as U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff plans and a cautious stance from Federal Reserve policymakers hurt risk sentiment.

FOREIGN MINISTER

Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan will attend a G20 foreign ministers meeting in Johannesburg.

TRANSPORT MINISTER

Transport Minister Abdulkadir Uraloglu will attend an event of a university in Ankara (0700 GMT).

OPPOSITION

Main opposition CHP leader Ozgur Ozel will visit jailed mayors, a journalist and the leader of another small opposition party who were detained in the latest investigations against opposition figures in Istanbul (1200 GMT).

CONSUMER CONFIDENCE (TRCONC=ECI)

The Turkish Statistics Institute will announce February consumer confidence index data (0700 GMT).

DEBT STOCK

Turkish treasury will announce debt stock for January (1430 GMT).

CENTRAL BANK DATA

The Central Bank will release its weekly data, including on forex reserves (1130 GMT).

BUSINESS GROUP PROBE

An Istanbul court banned on Thursday international travel by two key executives of Turkey's top business association in an investigation of their remarks on democracy that President Tayyip Erdogan characterised as undermining the government.

EGG EXPORT

Turkey has begun exporting around 15,000 tonnes of eggs to the United States as a devastating outbreak of bird flu is slashing U.S. production and sending prices soaring, a leading sector official said on Wednesday.

IRAQ PIPELINE

Turkey has yet to receive confirmation from Iraq on the resumption of oil flows through the Iraq-Turkey pipeline, Turkish Energy Minister Alparslan Bayraktar told Reuters on Wednesday.

data.title: Wesfarmers Will Continue to Evaluate Business Case for Lithium Expansion, CEO Says
Wesfarmers Has Capacity to Expand Lithium Project, CEO Says
Wesfarmers Focused on Ramping Up Lithium Refinery, CEO Says — Interview
data.description:
(05:10 GMT) *Wesfarmers Has Capacity to Expand Lithium Project, CEO Says
(05:10 GMT) *Wesfarmers Will Continue to Evaluate Business Case for Lithium Expansion, CEO Says
(05:10 GMT) *Target Customers for Lithium Project Are Non-Chinese Battery, Car Manufacturers, CEO Says
(05:10 GMT) Wesfarmers Focused on Ramping Up Lithium Refinery, CEO Says — Interview

By Mike Cherney

Australian retail-industrial conglomerate Wesfarmers is focused on ramping up a refinery at its lithium joint venture in Western Australia, and a possible expansion of the project is still on the table despite a drop in lithium prices, Chief Executive Rob Scott said in an interview.

"Over the last couple of years, we have acknowledged that we have the capacity to expand the project, given that we have a very high-quality and long-life mine," Scott told The Wall Street Journal. "But we've been very consistent in saying that we'll continue to evaluate the business case."

"There's certainly no change to how we would assess that expansion," he added, noting that getting the refinery commissioned and ramping up production is the company's first-order priority.

Prices for lithium, needed for electric-vehicle batteries, have experienced a sharp fall from record highs over the past two years. But producers continue to plan new projects, betting the metal will be in demand for EVs and energy storage for decades to come.

Wesfarmers's lithium project, a joint venture with Chilean mining company SQM, includes a mine and concentrator that produce a raw form called spodumene, plus a refinery that will make battery-grade lithium hydroxide.

Wesfarmers, which reported half-year earnings Thursday, said earlier in the day that refinery construction was 95% complete, commissioning was about 50% complete and that it expects its first product from the refinery in mid-calendar year 2025.

Like many critical minerals, China plays an outsize role in processing lithium. But extra tariffs on Chinese imports from President Trump could make lithium refined in other countries more attractive to customers seeking to limit exposure to China in their supply chains.

"Our target customer base are essentially non-Chinese battery manufacturers and car manufacturers," Scott said. "Whatever happens with the tariffs, we would expect our lithium would be sought after by a lot of non-Chinese producers."

Although there is uncertainty about how tariffs would impact the global economy, Scott told reporters earlier in the day that he didn't think tariffs would directly affect Wesfarmers's lithium business. When asked if the lithium business would make an adequate return if fully operational, he said it would.

"If we just look at our concentrator, would we be profitable selling spodumene at the market price when our concentrator is fully ramped? The answer to that would be yes," he told reporters. "Would we expect to be profitable through our lithium hydroxide refinery while it is ramped up based on the contracts we have and where the market's at? Yes we would."

"Clearly, if there were improvements in the future in price, then that would be beneficial," he added.

In 2023, Wesfarmers said its share of capital expenditure for the project would be 1.2 billion Australian dollars to A$1.3 billion, equivalent to US$761.4 million to US$824.8 million, excluding capitalized interest.

Scott was speaking after Wesfarmers reported a 2.9% rise in its half-year profit. The result was driven by a strong performance in its Bunnings and Kmart retail chains, which make up the bulk of Wesfarmers's earnings, though the company often invests in other businesses that it believes have strong growth prospects.

Write to Mike Cherney at mike.cherney@wsj.com

data.title: S.F. Holding's January Revenue Rises 8%
data.description:
S.F. Holding's revenue increased 7.9% to 26.3 billion yuan in January from 24.4 billion yuan in the year-ago period, according to a Wednesday filing with the Hong Kong Exchange.

Revenue from its express logistics business rose 6.5% to 20.8 billion yuan from 19.5 billion yuan.

On a per-parcel basis, revenue slipped 8.2% to 15.61 yuan from 17 yuan a year earlier. Parcel volume jumped 16% to 1.33 billion from 1.15 billion a year earlier.

Revenue from its supply chain and international business operations jumped 14% to 5.51 billion yuan from 4.86 billion yuan.

Shares in S.F. Holding rose 2% in Shenzhen and 1% in Hong Kong during Thursday's afternoon trading.

data.title: Cambi Books Order for Thermal Hydrolysis Process System in Spain
data.description:
Cambi said Wednesday it received an order for a thermal hydrolysis process system from a consortium comprising Espina, Obrascon, and Aquambiente.

The system will be installed between two digestion stages at the new Silvouta wastewater treatment plant in Santiago de Compostela, Spain.

The Norwegian waste treatment technology company expects to deliver the system in 2026.

data.title: Motivelink makes debuts strong on Kosdaq
data.description: Electronic auto components manufacturer Motivelink Co. more than doubled its initial public offering (IPO) price on Thursday, its first trading day on the Kosdaq. Motivelink shares traded at 15,910 won ($11.05) as of 9:15 a.m., up 165.17 percent from the IPO price of 6,000 won, after briefly hitting the 16,780-won mark at the open. The company develops and mass-produces transformers for automotive applications, supplying key components for eco-friendly vehicle systems. The stock recorded a competition rate of 1,075.5 to 1 during its institutional investor demand forecast, with demand soaring to 1,667.23 to 1 during the public subscription phase, attracting 3.78 trillion won in deposits. Meanwhile, shipbuilding stocks plunged in morning trading on the same day as brokerages downgraded ratings due to valuation concerns. HD Hyundai Heavy Industries Co. dropped 7.64 percent to 320,500 won as of 9:27 a.m., extending its early decline of 4.90 percent. Hanwha Ocean Co. fell 6.39 percent to 73,200 won, while HD Korea Shipbuilding & Offshore Engineering Co. slid 5.86 percent to 225,000 won. Other shipbuilders, including Samsung Heavy Industries Co., HD Hyundai Mipo Co., and HJ Shipbuilding & Construction Co., also saw sharp losses, dropping 4.55 percent, 5.58 percent, and 5.45 percent respectively. Shipbuilding stocks had rallied since Donald Trump’s election victory in 2024 on expectations of energy policy shifts and increased defense spending, but recent concerns over valuations have tempered gains. Hanwha Investment & Securities downgraded its ratings on HD Hyundai Heavy Industries and Hanwha Ocean from “Buy” to “Neutral” on the day, citing limited growth potential. The firm noted that South Korean shipbuilders could realistically secure only $193.4 billion - 16.1 percent of the total addressable market - for new U.S. Navy vessels over the next 30 years. In contrast, entertainment, cosmetics, and travel stocks surged on speculation that China may lift its ban on Korean pop culture as early as May 2025. SM Entertainment Co. jumped 9.22 percent to 105,400 won as of 9:18 a.m., while YG Entertainment Inc. (11.28 percent), JYP Entertainment Corp. (4.32 percent), and Hybe Co. (3.47 percent) also rallied. Hybe hit a 52-week high intraday. YG Plus Inc., which handles music distribution and intellectual property licensing for YG Entertainment, soared 17.18 percent. The content production sector also saw strong gains on expectations of increased copyright sales. Astory Co. surged 30 percent, while ContentreeJoongAng Corp. (23.13 percent), Studio Dragon Corp. (18.44 percent), Samhwa Networks Co. (13.63 percent), and CJ ENM Co. (11.03 percent) posted double-digit gains. Cosmetics stocks rose on optimism over export expansion, with Hankook Cosmetics Manufacturing Co. up 8.22 percent, Amorepacific Corp. 4.84 percent, and LG Household & Health Care Co. 4.60 percent. Travel stocks also climbed, with SM Culture & Contents Co. (6.42 percent), Yellow Balloon Tour Co. (4.49 percent), and Very Good Tour Co. (3.46 percent) gaining ground. A report earlier suggested that the Chinese government could lift its Hallyu Ban as soon as May 2025. While previous speculations have failed to materialize, market sentiment is leaning toward a higher likelihood this time. Analysts also see an increased likelihood and advise monitoring the long-term impact of China’s potential market reopening on related stocks.

data.title: Novautek Technologies Group Expects Wider Loss in Fiscal H1
data.description:
Novautek Technologies Group expects a loss of about HK$70.9 million for the fiscal first half ended Dec. 31, 2024, wider than the loss of HK$35.6 million the previous year, according to Wednesday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The company attributed the expected increase in loss to an increase in fair value loss of investments in properties, financial assets, and impairment losses in property.

The resort and property business developer will publish the interim results by Feb. 25.

The company shares were down by about 8% in recent trading.

data.title: Devyser Diagnostics Wins Contract to Supply Comprehensive Genetic Sequencing Tests to Italy
data.description:
Devyser Diagnostics said Wednesday it secured the contract worth 16.8 million kronor for the supply of its comprehensive next-generation sequencing tests to Torino, Italy.

The university hospital AOU Città Della Salute e della Scienza awarded the three-year contract, which covers CFTR, BRCA and Thalassemia genetic testing, the Swedish genetic testing solutions provider noted.

data.title: Bank Indonesia Could Cut Rates to 5.25% This Year, UOB Says — Market Talk
data.description: Bank Indonesia could cut rates twice by 25 bps each in 2Q and 3Q, bringing the policy rate to 5.25% where it could remain for the rest of 2025, UOB economists Enrico Tanuwidjaja and Vincentius Ming Shen say in a note. Based on BI's recent signals, it remains optimistic about Indonesia's economic resilience amid steady consumption and investment demand, despite internal and external headwinds, they note. They think BI's stance could indicate the potential for further cuts, but the timing will depend on economic conditions. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Fathopes Energy, Topsoe Forge Partnership To Advance Sustainable Aviation Fuel Production In Malaysia
data.description:
FATHOPES ENERGY:

  • FATHOPES ENERGY AND TOPSOE FORGE PARTNERSHIP TO ADVANCE SUSTAINABLE AVIATION FUEL PRODUCTION IN MALAYSIA

data.title: CECEP Solar Energy's Unit to Build Fishing Farm Solar Generator for 62 Million Yuan
data.description:
CECEP Solar Energy's unit, CEC Solar Technology Harbin, has earmarked 61.7 million yuan to build a distributed photovoltaic power generation composite project in Wuchang City, Heilongjiang Province, China, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Thursday.

The 11.9-megawatt solar power generator will be used in a fishing farm, the solar panel manufacturer said.

data.title: Electronic Arts Inc. Being Investigated on Behalf of Electronic Arts Inc. Investors. Contact Levi & Korsinsky For Details.
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 19, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Electronic Arts Inc. ("Electronic Arts Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On January 22, 2025, EA issued a press release pre-announcing their preliminary Q3 FY25 results and announcing a guide-down from the company's prior expectations. Where "EA's initial guidance for fiscal year 2025 anticipated mid-single-digit growth in live services net bookings … the company now projects a mid-single-digit decline." EA primarily pointed to two setbacks: Global Football, specifically EA Sports FC 25, which required "a comprehensive gameplay refresh" earlier this month; and Dragon Age, which engaged approximately 1.5 million players during the quarter, down nearly 50% from the company's expectations."

Following this news, EA's stock price fell by $24.12 per share and is currently trading at $118.23 per share. To obtain additional information, go to:

data.title: Shareholder Rights Advocates at Levi & Korsinsky Investigate Olin Corporation (OLN) Regarding Possible Securities Fraud Violations
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 19, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Olin Corporation ("Olin Corporation") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

Olin issued a press on January 30, 2025, reporting its financial results for the fourth quarter of 2024. Olin also issued guidance for the first quarter of 2025, including adjusted EBITDA of $150 million to $170 million, falling well short of the $227.1 million analyst consensus estimate. Olin cited "challenging industry conditions" "as commercial customers continue to work down elevated inventories along with softer consumer demand," global epoxy demand remains weak, and its U.S. and European epoxy business "remains significantly challenged by subsidized Asian competition."

Following this news, Olin's stock price fell over 8% on January 31, 2025. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/olin-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=130786&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

data.title: Grieg Seafood reports Q4 and preliminary results for 2024 – restructures and refinances to refocus on profitable growth in Norway
data.description:
Figures for Q4 2023 in brackets

  • Transformation program
    • Reallocating resources to focus on profitable growth in Norway
    • Lowering growth ambitions and investment levels in Canada significantly
    • Continuing and sharpening operational and financial improvement programs
    • Contemplating NOK 1.5 - 2.0 bn hybrid bond to strengthen equity and financial flexibility
    • Progressive dialogue regarding the sale-leaseback of Adamselv smolt / post-smolt facilities in Finnmark
  • Financial results
    • Harvest volume of 23,551 tonnes (21,767 tonnes) in Q4 and 77,704 tonnes (72,015 tonnes) for the full year
    • Operational EBIT loss of NOK 74 million (NOK -67 million) in Q4, corresponding to a loss of NOK 3.1 per kg (-3.1)
    • Operational EBIT of NOK 8 million for the full year 2024 (NOK 780 million), corresponding to NOK 0.1 per kg (10.8)
    • Recognized impairments of NOK 1.7 billion related to the Canadian operations
  • Outlook
    • Demand and supply dynamics support sustained good market
    • Entered 2025 close to MAB capacity in both Rogaland and Finnmark - expecting 14% growth in harvest volumes in Norway in 2025
    • Continued cautious approach in British Columbia and reduced growth outlook in Newfoundland - expecting 5% decline in harvest volumes in Canada in 2025.
    • Expect harvest volumes of 18,800 tonnes in Q1 2025 and target 84,000 tonnes in 2025

(Bergen, 20 February 2025) Grieg Seafood launches a transformation program to lay the financial and organizational foundation for profitable growth and continued development of its Norwegian assets in Rogaland and Finnmark. The company entered 2025 with close to maximum allowed biomass (MAB) in both regions, and expects 14% growth in harvesting volumes to 62,000 tonnes.

In Canada, the company is lowering its growth ambitions and related capital expenditure plans significantly in Newfoundland and has also put strategic investments on hold in British Columbia while awaiting conclusions on the outlined transition for salmon farming in the region. As a result, the company expects harvesting volumes in Canada to decline by around 5% to 22,000 tonnes in 2025.

Changes in operational, industry, economic and regulatory conditions have led to changes in future plans, assumptions and estimates for the Canadian operations, and the company has recognized impairment losses in excess of NOK 1.7 billion to reflect these changes. Approximately NOK 1 billion of the impairment relates to intangible assets and approximately NOK 0.7 billion to property, plant, and equipment.

Moving forward, Grieg Seafood is executing a set of financial initiatives to strengthen the balance sheet. This enables the required financial flexibility and liquidity to pursue continued growth. The transition captures the true value of our Norwegian asset base and preserves the value of our Canadian assets. This includes the contemplated issuance of a hybrid bond, sale-leaseback of the smolt/post-smolt facilities in Adamselv in Finnmark, and a further sharpening of the company’s ongoing improvement programs to enhance operational stability and improve cost efficiency.

- We are currently not capturing the value potential that lies in our assets and our operations, and we need to manage a financial and operational transformation to realize these values going forward. We are now reallocating resources to continue developing our Norwegian assets, while scaling down our growth ambitions and related CAPEX investments for our Canadian operations. Hereby, we are taking upfront action to strengthen our balance sheet and financial flexibility, and doubling down on our operational and financial improvement programs, says CEO Andreas Kvame.

The executed and planned transactions are expected to have a combined positive liquidity effect of approximately NOK 2.5 billion (before financing costs and green bond repayment in June 2025) as well as reducing NIBD by approximately NOK 1.5 - 2.2 billion and will greatly enhance the financial flexibility going forward.

- We have a fundamentally strong asset base, and with operational improvements and financial discipline we are going to drive sustainable and profitable growth and shareholder value creation going forward, says Andreas Kvame.

Financial results

While the operational performance in Rogaland remained strong, operations in Finnmark were negatively impacted by string jellyfish, triggering a cost increase of NOK 75 million in the quarter. With additional negative EBIT from the Canadian operations, Grieg Seafood reports an operational loss of NOK 74 million, compared to an operational EBIT loss of NOK 67 million in the fourth quarter 2023. Operational EBIT/kg was NOK -3.1 (-3.1).

For the full year 2024, the operational EBIT amounted to NOK 8 million, compared to NOK 780 million in 2023, corresponding to an operational EBIT/kg of NOK 0.1 in 2024 and NOK 10.8 in 2023. The total harvest volume was 77,704 tonnes, up from 72,015 tonnes in 2023.

Recognizing impairments of NOK 1,737 million related to the Canadian operations, the company reports an EBIT-loss of NOK 1,561 million fourth quarter and an EBIT-loss of NOK 2,310 million for the full year 2024.

Outlook

Demand for Atlantic salmon is expected to remain strong, with a persisting focus on healthy foods and sustainable produced proteins. Even though the global supply of Atlantic salmon is expected to increase somewhat from 2024 to 2025, this should support a sustained robust market.

The company entered the year with a biomass close to the MAB capacity and expects to see increasing harvesting volumes in Norway in 2025. Given the uncertain political climate, the company is not planning for increased harvesting in Canada.

The potential imposition of tariffs on goods exports into the US adds uncertainty, particularly with regards to exports from the company’s operations in Canada.

The company expects harvest volumes of 18,800 tonnes in Q1 2025, distributed as follows:

  • Rogaland: 6,500 tonnes
  • Finnmark: 7,600 tonnes
  • British Columbia: 0 tonnes
  • Newfoundland: 4,700 tonnes

Estimated contract share for the Norwegian operations is 23% for Q1 2025.

For the full year 2025, the company expects harvest volumes of 84,000 tonnes, distributed as follows:

  • Rogaland: 30,000 tonnes
  • Finnmark: 32,000 tonnes
  • British Columbia: 12,000 tonnes
  • Newfoundland: 10,000 tonnes

Results presentation

CEO Andreas Kvame and CFO Magnus Johannesen will present the results live at Hotel Continental, Stortingsgaten 24/26, Oslo today at 08:00 a.m. CET.

A live webcast from the presentation can be accessed at www.investor.griegseafood.com or with the following link: https://channel.royalcast.com/landingpage/hegnarmedia/20250220_2/

An English transcript of the presentation will be made available at www.investor.griegseafood.com.

For further enquiries, please contact:

Andreas Kvame, CEO

Cell phone: +47 907 71 441

Magnus Johannesen, CFO

Cell phone: +47 959 13 243

About Grieg Seafood

Grieg Seafood ASA is one of the world's leading salmon farmers. Our farms are in Finnmark and Rogaland in Norway, and British Columbia as well as Newfoundland in Canada. Our headquarter is located in Bergen, Norway. Grieg Seafood ASA was listed at the Oslo Stock Exchange in June 2007.

Sustainable farming practices are the foundation of Grieg Seafood's operations. The lowest possible environmental impact and the best possible fish welfare is both an ethical responsibility and drive economic profitability.

To learn more, please visit www.griegseafood.com.

The information included in this announcement may partly be defined as inside information pursuant to article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation. The information is publicly disclosed in accordance with article 17 of the Market Abuse Regulation as well as section 5-12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act. The stock exchange announcement was published by May Hansen, Administrative Secretary, Grieg Seafood Group ASA, on 20 February 2025 at 6:00 a.m. CET.

data.title: Grieg Seafood ASA: Contemplating perpetual green hybrid bond issue
data.description:
(Bergen, 20 February 2025) Grieg Seafood ASA (the “Company”) has mandated Arctic Securities, DNB Markets and Nordea as joint bookrunners, and DNB and Nordea as green bond advisors, to arrange a series of fixed income meetings commencing Friday, 21 February 2025. The Company is contemplating the issuance of a new perpetual green hybrid bond of NOK 1,500 - 2,000 million, subject to inter alia market conditions.

Net proceeds from the bond issue will be used for green projects as further defined by the Green Bond Framework, including by way of refinancing existing debt originally incurred to finance such green projects.

In conjunction with the contemplated new bond issue, the company will offer a conditional buyback of its outstanding bond with ticker GSF01 ESG (ISIN: NO0010885007).

For further enquiries, please contact:

Magnus Johannesen, CFO

Cell phone +47 959 13 243

About Grieg Seafood

Grieg Seafood ASA is one of the world's leading salmon farmers. Our farms are in Finnmark and Rogaland in Norway, and British Columbia as well as Newfoundland in Canada. Our headquarter is located in Bergen, Norway. Grieg Seafood ASA was listed at the Oslo Stock Exchange in June 2007.

Sustainable farming practices are the foundation of Grieg Seafood's operations. The lowest possible environmental impact and the best possible fish welfare is both an ethical responsibility and drive economic profitability.

To learn more, please visit www.griegseafood.com

The information included in this announcement is publicly disclosed in accordance with section 5-12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.
